data.title: Tribeca Resources Initiates Drilling at Chiricuto Property in Chile
data.description:
Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Hansa Resources ( (TSE:TRBC) ) has shared an announcement.

Tribeca Resources has started its maiden drilling program at the Chiricuto Property in Chile’s Atacama region, following extensive geological mapping, geophysics, and soil sampling. The drilling aims to explore two targets validated through surveys, with approximately 1,500 meters of planned drilling to test for iron oxide copper-gold mineralization, potentially enhancing the company’s position in the copper industry.

More about Hansa Resources

Tribeca Resources Corporation is a company focused on developing copper mines in Chile. Their primary focus is on exploring and developing properties with significant mineral potential, particularly in regions dominated by existing copper projects.

YTD Price Performance: -6.67%

Average Trading Volume: 12,542

Technical Sentiment Consensus Rating: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$14.48M

data.description:

NEXT RE SIIQ S.p.A. ( (IT:NR) ) has shared an announcement.

Next Re SIIQ S.p.A. has announced its preliminary financial data for the fiscal year 2024, marking a significant turnaround with a profit of €1.61 million, compared to a loss of €9.44 million in the previous year. The company’s preliminary EBITDA increased to €2.06 million from a negative €0.64 million, and its financial debt showed a positive shift by reducing significantly, highlighting improvements due to asset reclassification and strategic sales, indicating a more sustainable financial structure.

More about NEXT RE SIIQ S.p.A.

Next Re SIIQ S.p.A. is a real estate investment company listed on the Euronext Milan market of Borsa Italiana.

WH Smith ( (GB:SMWH) ) has issued an update.

WH Smith PLC announced the repurchase of 22,250 of its own shares as part of its ongoing buyback programme. This move is aimed at enhancing shareholder value by reducing the number of shares in circulation, thereby potentially increasing the value of remaining shares. Since the start of the programme, the company has repurchased a total of 1,477,628 shares. The decision to cancel these shares could signify WH Smith’s confidence in its financial position and commitment to returning value to its shareholders.

More about WH Smith

WH Smith PLC operates in the retail industry, focusing primarily on the sale of books, stationery, magazines, and convenience products. The company has a notable presence in travel and high street locations, catering to a broad range of consumers.

The latest announcement is out from Obiz SA ( (FR:ALBIZ) ).

Obiz announced its share and voting rights information as of January 31, 2025, reflecting its compliance with the Financial Markets Authority’s regulations. The company has 5,872,132 shares and varying numbers of theoretical and exercisable voting rights. This announcement may impact stakeholders as the company continues to strengthen its market positioning through innovation and strategic partnerships.

More about Obiz SA

Obiz, established in 2010, is a leader in responsible relationship marketing. It offers innovative solutions designed to enhance the attractiveness and competitiveness of large companies, associations, and federations through a proprietary digital platform. With operations in 65 countries and partnerships with over 55,000 national and local commercial entities, Obiz manages more than 150 programs benefiting 35 million people. The company achieved a turnover of 83.1 million euros in 2023, reflecting a significant growth of 108%, and it is listed on the Euronext Growth market in Paris.

data.title: Roctool’s Advanced Assembly Process Revolutionizes Photovoltaic Panel Production
data.description:
Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

An announcement from RocTool SA ( (FR:ALROC) ) is now available.

Roctool, in collaboration with CEA-Liten, has developed the LIT™ RAPID SOLAR thermo-compression equipment, which enhances the assembly of photovoltaic panels by enabling the use of new thermoplastic polymer materials and complex 3D formats. This innovation allows for higher temperature and pressure conditions than standard processes, potentially improving recycling capabilities and expanding design possibilities in the photovoltaic industry.

More about RocTool SA

Roctool specializes in rapid heating and cooling technologies for plastic injection and composite molding, serving industries such as automotive, electronics, consumer goods, renewable energy, luxury and beauty packaging, and medical. The company provides engineering services, induction generators, tooling equipment, and on-site support, with operations in the US, China, Japan, and Germany.

data.title: GCP Infra Announces Share Buyback to Optimize Capital Structure
data.description:
Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

GCP Infra Invt Shs GBP ( (GB:GCP) ) has issued an update.

GCP Infrastructure Investments Ltd has announced the repurchase of 375,000 of its own ordinary shares at an average price of 73.23 pence each, as part of the authority granted by its shareholders to make market purchases. This transaction, executed on February 6, 2025, results in the company holding a total of 22,385,019 shares in treasury, with 862,412,650 shares remaining in circulation for voting purposes. The move reflects GCP Infra’s strategy to manage its capital structure efficiently, potentially impacting shareholder value and market perception.

More about GCP Infra Invt Shs GBP

GCP Infra is a closed-ended investment company listed on the London Stock Exchange and a constituent of the FTSE-250. The company focuses on providing shareholders with long-term, sustainable distributions by investing in UK infrastructure debt and related assets, particularly those with public sector-backed, availability-based revenues. It also aims to offer partial inflation protection where possible and is recognized for contributing to positive environmental outcomes, as evidenced by its London Stock Exchange Green Economy Mark.

data.description:

Downing Renewables & Infrastructure Trust Plc ( (GB:DORE) ) has shared an update.

Downing Renewables & Infrastructure Trust Plc announced the purchase of 50,000 of its own ordinary shares, which will be held in treasury. This transaction affects the total voting rights, now standing at 171,191,178 shares in circulation. This move is part of DORE’s strategy to manage its share capital effectively, potentially impacting shareholder calculations under FCA rules and reflecting the company’s focus on stability and sustainable growth.

More about Downing Renewables & Infrastructure Trust Plc

Downing Renewables & Infrastructure Trust Plc (DORE) is a closed-end investment trust focused on providing sustainable income and capital growth through investments in renewable energy and infrastructure assets in the UK and Northern Europe. The company has received recognition for its contributions to the green economy, including the Green Economy Mark from the London Stock Exchange and the Renewables Fund of the Year award.
